summary: Implemented fixed whole-GameDay scheduler, deterministic versioned system order, integer calendar boundaries, 3650-day headless advancement, scheduler hash participation, static authority-time guard enhancement, and sim-core performance baseline command.

tests_run.result: Node hash and worker-compatible hash both 61e0ba1c for 30 days.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.
Architecture check and determinism passed; root Vitest 3 files/17 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.
minimal-m1-world-state-v0, 3650 days, finalDay 3650, finalRevision 3650, throughput 67283 days/sec, p50 10100 ns, p95 30600 ns, p99 77000 ns on node v24.13.1 win32 x64 cpuCount=16.

decisions: R3 touched: WorldState v0 scheduler fields and canonical hash input now include schedulerVersion, systemOrderVersion, fixedStepDurationInDays, lastCompletedDay, and pendingCommandCount.
M1 scheduler uses integer-only 30-day months and 12-month years for deterministic day/month/year boundary coverage; no Date or host clock is used in authoritative sim-core source.
Performance timing uses Node hrtime only in packages/sim-core/scripts/performance-baseline.mjs, outside authoritative sim-core src.
risks: Existing project/* working-tree changes and SIM-002 route file are orchestration artifacts and not part of the simulation implementation.
QA should review the architecture guard carve-out for packages/sim-core/scripts so package tooling can use Node timing while authoritative src remains guarded.
requested_action: Final QA review SIM-002 fixed GameDay scheduler implementation, deterministic system order, calendar/3650-day tests, hash participation, static guard behavior, and performance baseline output.
